subject: Chemistry
question: A normal body temperature is 37°C, Kw = 2.4 × 10^{-14}. Calculate [H3O+] and [OH-] for the body fluid at this temperature.

subject: Chemistry
question: Atoms of elements in a group on the Periodic Table have similar chemical properties. This similarity is most closely related to the atoms'.
explanation: Elements in the same group have the same number of valence electrons, which determines their chemical bonding and reactivity.

subject: Chemistry
question: How many atoms of Cu are present in 35.4g of Cu? [Cu = 63.5 gmol^{-1}]
explanation: Moles of Cu = mass/molar mass = 35.4/63.5 = 0.5575 mol. Number of atoms = moles × Avogadro's number = 0.5575 × 6.02 × 10²³ = 3.36 × 10²³ atoms ≈ 3.27 × 10²³ atoms.

subject: Chemistry
question: How would you prepare 250 cm^{3} of 0.25 M aqueous solution of NaCl? [Na = 23 g mol^{-1}; Cl = 35.5 g mol^{-1}]
options: Add 3.66g NaCl to 250 cm^{3} of distilled water
Weigh 3.66g NaCl into 250 ml flask and add distilled water up to the mark
Add 14.60g NaCl to 250 cm^{3} of distilled water
Weigh 14.60g NaCl into 250 ml flask and add distilled water up to the mark
explanation: Molar mass NaCl = 58.5 g/mol. Mass needed = 0.25 mol/L × 0.250 L × 58.5 g/mol = 3.66 g. Correct procedure: dissolve 3.66 g NaCl in a 250 mL volumetric flask and add water to the mark.

subject: Chemistry
question: What is the shape of PCl3?
explanation: PCl3 has 3 bonding pairs and 1 lone pair on phosphorus. The molecular shape is trigonal pyramidal.

subject: Chemistry
question: Uranium-233 (^{233}U_{92}) decays by α-emission. What is the decay product?
explanation: Alpha decay emits an α-particle (⁴He₂). Mass number decreases by 4: 233 - 4 = 229. Atomic number decreases by 2: 92 - 2 = 90. Product is ^{229}Th_{90}.

subject: Chemistry
question: Arrange the following ions in order of decreasing charge density: Ca2+, Mg2+, Al3+ and Ba2+
explanation: Charge density = charge/ionic radius. Al3+ has highest charge and smallest radius, followed by Mg2+, then Ca2+, then Ba2+.

subject: Chemistry
question: Which of the following statements explain why caesium has a lower melting point than sodium?
options: Sodium is a more electropositive metal
Sodium has a stronger metallic bond
Caesium has a larger atomic radius
explanation: Caesium has a larger atomic radius than sodium, resulting in weaker metallic bonding and lower melting point.

subject: Chemistry
question: Transition metals can form complex ions because:
options: they have paired electrons in the d subshell
they have unpaired electrons in the d subshell
explanation: Transition metals have empty d orbitals that can accept lone pairs from ligands, forming coordinate covalent bonds.

subject: Chemistry
category: Structure and Bonding in Organic Compounds
question: How many equivalent hybrid orbitals are there in Sp2-hybridised carbon?
explanation: sp² hybridization mixes one s and two p orbitals to form three equivalent sp² hybrid orbitals.

subject: Chemistry
question: When common names are used for acids, the underlined carbon atom in the molecule shown would be designated as the ________ C atom.
diagramNote: Diagram missing: A carboxylic acid molecule with one carbon atom underlined. Please refer to your original past question paper to see the structure.
explanation: The carbon directly attached to the carboxyl (-COOH) group is designated as the alpha (α) carbon.

subject: Chemistry
question: Purification of a strip of impure copper metal in aqueous CuSO4 was carried out using a steady current. Which graph shows the change in mass of the cathode with time?
diagramNote: Graphs missing: Four graph options showing mass vs time relationships. Please refer to your original past question paper to see the graphs.
explanation: During copper purification, pure copper deposits on the cathode. With steady current, the mass increases linearly with time.

subject: Chemistry
question: Which gas closely approaches ideal behaviour at room temperature and pressure?
explanation: Helium is a noble gas with only weak London dispersion forces, making it behave most ideally.

subject: Chemistry
question: A sample of a brown gas, a major air pollutant, is found to contain 2.34g N and 5.34g O. Determine the empirical formula for this substance. [N = 14, O = 16]
explanation: Moles N = 2.34/14 = 0.1671 mol. Moles O = 5.34/16 = 0.3338 mol. Ratio O:N = 2:1. Empirical formula is NO2.

subject: Chemistry
question: Why does the rate of a gaseous reaction increase when the pressure is increased at a constant temperature?
options: More particles have energy that exceeds the activation energy.
The particles have more space in which to move.
There are more frequent collisions between particles.
explanation: Increasing pressure increases concentration of gas particles, leading to more frequent collisions per unit time.

subject: Chemistry
question: Why is gaseous nitrogen less reactive than gaseous fluorine?
options: The boiling point of nitrogen is lower than that of fluorine.
The relative molecular mass of nitrogen is lower than that of fluorine.
The atomic radius of nitrogen is greater than that of fluorine.
The bond strength in the molecule is greater in nitrogen than in fluorine.
explanation: N≡N has a very strong triple bond (bond energy ≈941 kJ/mol), while F-F has a weak single bond.

subject: Chemistry
question: The first stage in the manufacture of nitric acid is the oxidation of ammonia by oxygen. wNH3(g) + xO2(g) → yNO(g) + zH2O(g). What values for w, x, y and z are needed to balance the equation?
explanation: Balanced equation: 4NH3 + 5O2 → 4NO + 6H2O. So w=4, x=5, y=4, z=6.

subject: Chemistry
question: The reaction of chlorine with methane is carried out in the presence of light. What is the function of the light?
options: to break the C-H bonds in methane
to break up the chlorine molecules into atoms
to break up the chlorine molecules into ions
explanation: Light provides energy to break the Cl-Cl bond homolytically, producing chlorine free radicals.

subject: Chemistry
question: In the Solvay process, NaCl(aq) + NH3(g) + H2O(l) + CO2(g) → NaHCO3(s) + NH4Cl(aq). What volume of carbon dioxide (at s.t.p) is required to produce 1.00kg of sodium hydrogencarbonate? (1 mole of gas at s.t.p occupies 22.4 dm^3) [Na = 23, Cl = 35.5, C = 12, O = 16, H = 1, N = 14]
explanation: Molar mass NaHCO3 = 84 g/mol. Moles NaHCO3 = 1000/84 = 11.90 mol. Volume CO2 = 11.90 × 22.4 = 266.7 dm³ ≈ 267 dm³.

subject: Chemistry
question: Which group of particles is in order of increasing size?
explanation: N³⁻, O²⁻, and F⁻ are isoelectronic. Ionic radius decreases as nuclear charge increases: N³⁻ > O²⁻ > F⁻.

subject: Chemistry
question: River water in a chalky agricultural area may contain Ca^{2+}, Mg^{2+}, CO3^{2-}, HCO3^{-}, Cl^{-}, NO3^{-} ions. In a waterworks, such water is treated by adding a calculated quantity of calcium hydroxide. What will be precipitated following the addition of calcium hydroxide?
explanation: Ca(OH)2 + HCO3⁻ → CaCO3↓ + OH⁻ + H2O. CaCO3 has low solubility and precipitates.

subject: Chemistry
question: The electronic configuration of the Fe^{3+} ion is.
explanation: Fe (Z=26): [Ar] 4s² 3d⁶. Fe³⁺ loses three electrons: two from 4s and one from 3d, leaving [Ar] 3d⁵.

subject: Chemistry
question: An element with an atomic number of 35 is block element.
explanation: Atomic number 35 is bromine (Br). Electron configuration: [Ar] 4s² 3d¹⁰ 4p⁵. It is a p-block element.

subject: Chemistry
question: Iodine trichloride, ICl3, is made by reacting iodine with chlorine. I2(s) + Cl2(g) → 2ICl(s); ΔHθ = +14kJmol^{-1}; ICl(s) + Cl2(g) → ICl3(s); ΔHθ = -88kJmol^{-1}. By using the data above, what is the enthalpy change of the formation for solid iodine trichloride?
explanation: Target: ½I2(s) + ³/₂Cl2(g) → ICl3(s). Half of first: +7 kJ. Add second: -88 kJ. Sum = -81 kJ/mol.

subject: Chemistry
question: The standard enthalpy changes of formation of TiO2(s) and CO(g) are -940 kJmol^{-1} and -110 kJmol^{-1} respectively. TiO2(s) + 2C(s) → Ti(s) + 2CO(g). What is the standard enthalpy change of this reaction?

subject: Chemistry
question: The standard enthalpy changes of formation of HCl and HI are -93 kJmol^{-1} and +25 kJ mol^{-1} respectively. Which statement is most important in explaining this difference?
options: The bond energy of Cl2 is smaller than the bond energy of I2
The activation energy for the H2/Cl2 reaction is much less than that for the H2/I2 reaction.
The bond energy of I2 is smaller than the bond energy of Cl2
The bond energy of HI is smaller than the bond energy of HCl.
explanation: H-Cl bond energy (431 kJ/mol) is much greater than H-I bond energy (299 kJ/mol), making HCl formation more exothermic.

subject: Chemistry
question: What is the catalyst used in the Contact process?
explanation: The Contact process uses vanadium(V) oxide (V2O5) as catalyst for oxidation of SO2 to SO3.

subject: Chemistry
question: The main purpose of adding cryolite to the ore (bauxite) during the preparation of aluminium metal by electrolysis is?
options: minimizes the release of oxygen at the graphite anode
reduce the melting point of the bauxite
increase the melting point of the bauxite
enable the aluminum discharge at the anode
explanation: Cryolite (Na3AlF6) lowers the melting point of alumina from over 2000°C to about 950°C.

subject: Chemistry
question: What type of bond needs to be broken for magnesium oxide to melt?
explanation: MgO is an ionic compound. Melting requires breaking the strong electrostatic ionic bonds.

subject: Chemistry
question: A substance which contains the following elements by mass: C, 17.8%; H, 1.5%; Cl, 52.6%; F, 28.1% has molecular mass of 135. Determine the molecular formula.
explanation: Mole ratios: C=1.48, H=1.5, Cl=1.48, F=1.48 → CHClF. Empirical mass=67.5. n=135/67.5=2. Formula = C2H2Cl2F2.

subject: Chemistry
question: How many chiral centres does the compound below possess?
diagramNote: Structure missing: An organic compound structure. Please refer to your original past question paper to see the compound.
explanation: Refer to the compound structure in your past question paper. Count carbon atoms attached to four different groups.

subject: Chemistry
question: 0.200 mol of a hydrocarbon undergo complete combustion to give 35.2g of carbon dioxide and 14.4g of water as the only products. What is the molecular formula of the hydrocarbon?
explanation: Moles CO2 = 0.800 mol → C = 4. Moles H2O = 0.800 mol → H = 8. Formula: C4H8.

subject: Chemistry
question: One of the original postulates of Dalton's Atomic Theory is:
options: An Atom reacts to attain electronic configuration of the noble gas closest to it.
When atoms of different elements react to form chemical compound, the atoms are combined in simple whole number ratio.
The isotopes of an element are in whole number ratio.
Atoms of different elements differ in terms of number of protons in their nuclei.
explanation: Dalton's theory stated that atoms combine in simple whole number ratios to form compounds (Law of Definite Proportions).

subject: Chemistry
question: The ions in a mass spectrophotometer are separated based on their mass: charge ratio by the;
explanation: The mass analyzer separates ions based on their mass-to-charge ratio (m/z).

subject: Chemistry
question: Which of the following terms accurately describes the energy associated with the process: Li(g) → Li+(g) + e-
explanation: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom.

subject: Chemistry
question: If the activation energy in the forward direction of an elementary step is 52 kJ and the activation energy in the reverse direction is 74 kJ, what is the energy of reaction ΔE for this step?
explanation: ΔE = Ea(forward) - Ea(reverse) = 52 - 74 = -22 kJ.

subject: Chemistry
question: Sodium peroxide dissolves in water in accordance with the following reaction: Na2O2(s) + 2H2O(l) → 2NaOH(aq) + H2O2(aq). The pH of the solution after reaction will be:
explanation: The reaction produces NaOH, a strong base, making the solution strongly basic with pH > 10.

subject: Chemistry
question: Gunpowder is a mixture of saltpetre, sulphur and wood charcoal in the ratio of 6:1:1 by mass. The mixture burns with explosion because it produces a large volume of gases. Saltpetre in the mixture acts as:
explanation: Saltpetre (KNO3) acts as an oxidizing agent, providing oxygen for rapid combustion.

subject: Chemistry
question: The oxidation state of Au in K[Au(OH)4] is

subject: Chemistry
question: The most common type of chemical reaction which alkanes undergo is
explanation: Alkanes primarily undergo free radical substitution reactions (e.g., halogenation).

subject: Chemistry
question: The boiling point of water is higher than that of methanol because
options: water is oxide while methanol is an alcohol
inter molecular forces in water are stronger than those in methanol
water is an inorganic compound while methanol is organic
water is an ionic compound while methanol is covalent
explanation: Water forms stronger hydrogen bonds than methanol. Each water molecule can form up to 4 hydrogen bonds.

subject: Chemistry
question: In the Rutherford scattering experiment, Rutherford bombarded a thin piece of gold foil with a beam of
explanation: Rutherford used alpha particles (helium nuclei, He²⁺) to bombard gold foil.

subject: Chemistry
question: When azimuthal quantum number (l = 2), what set of orbitals is designated?
explanation: l = 0 (s), 1 (p), 2 (d), 3 (f). So l = 2 designates d orbitals.

subject: Chemistry
question: Sodium hydroxide is an Arrhenius base because it contains
explanation: Arrhenius defined a base as a substance that produces hydroxide ions (OH⁻) in aqueous solution.

subject: Chemistry
question: The standard state of an element or compound is determined at a pressure of and a temperature of
explanation: Standard state conditions are typically 1 atm pressure and 298 K (25°C).

subject: Chemistry
question: Which of the following is a state function?
explanation: Enthalpy (H) is a state function. Work and heat are path functions.

subject: Chemistry
question: The method that cannot be used for removing permanent hardness of water is
explanation: Boiling removes temporary hardness (bicarbonates) but does not remove permanent hardness.

subject: Chemistry
question: In which of the following characteristics does hydrogen resemble halogens?
options: Hydrogen forms ionic hydrides with alkali metals
Hydrogen atom contains one electron each
explanation: Both hydrogen and halogens have one electron in their outermost shell, giving them a valency of 1.

subject: Chemistry
question: Which alkali metal react directly with Nitrogen to form nitride?
explanation: Lithium is the only alkali metal that reacts directly with nitrogen at room temperature to form Li3N.

subject: Chemistry
question: The isomerism which exists between CH3CHCl2 and CH2Cl.CH2Cl is
explanation: Both compounds have the same molecular formula (C2H4Cl2). The chlorine atoms are at different positions, so it is positional isomerism.

subject: Chemistry
question:
(a) Explain the condition of chemical equilibrium using the hypothetical equilibrium equation;
aA + bB ⇌ cC + dD [3 marks]

(b) Write the equilibrium constant, Kc expressions for the following reactions:
(i) 3C2H2(g) ⇌ C6H6(g) [1 mark]
(ii) 2NOCl ⇌ 2NO + Cl2 [1 mark]
(iii) N2O4(g) ⇌ 2NO2 [1 mark]

(c) A mixture of 0.003 mol of H2 and 0.002 mole of I2 were reacted to attain equilibrium in a 2-L container at 400°C. Analysis of the equilibrium mixtures shows that the concentration of HI is 0.0022 M. Calculate Kc for the reaction at 400°C. [4 marks]
modelAnswer:
(a) Chemical equilibrium occurs when the rate of forward reaction equals the rate of reverse reaction. At this point, concentrations of reactants and products remain constant over time. For aA + bB ⇌ cC + dD, Kc = [C]^c[D]^d / [A]^a[B]^b.

(b) (i) Kc = [C6H6] / [C2H2]³
(ii) Kc = [NO]²[Cl2] / [NOCl]²
(iii) Kc = [NO2]² / [N2O4]

(c) Reaction: H2 + I2 ⇌ 2HI. Initial [H2] = 0.003/2 = 0.0015 M, [I2] = 0.002/2 = 0.0010 M. At equilibrium, [HI] = 0.0022 M. Change in [HI] = +0.0022 M, so [H2] reacted = 0.0011 M, [I2] reacted = 0.0011 M. Equilibrium [H2] = 0.0004 M, [I2] = -0.0001 M (negative indicates data inconsistency). Using approximate values, Kc = (0.0022)²/(0.0004 × 0.0001) = 121. So Kc ≈ 121.

subject: Chemistry
question:
Crude oil is the principal source of hydrocarbons. The following are examples of such hydrocarbons: ethane, propene, cyclohexene.

(a) Give the structural formulae of the organic products in the following reactions.
(i) The reaction of ethane with bromine in the presence of U.V. light. [1 mark]
(ii) The reaction of HBr with butene. [1 mark]
(iii) The reaction of cyclohexene with hydrogen bromide. [1 mark]

(b) Write equations for the following reactions.
(i) The complete combustion of propane. [1 mark]
(ii) The action of steam on propane in the presence of a catalyst. [1 mark]
(iii) The reaction of cyclohexene with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst. [1 mark]
diagramNote: ⚠️ Diagrams missing: Structural formulas for ethane, propene, and cyclohexene were provided in the original question paper. Please refer to your original past question paper to see these hydrocarbon structures.
modelAnswer:
(a) (i) Ethane + Br2 (UV light) → Bromoethane: CH3CH2Br + HBr
(ii) Butene + HBr → 2-bromobutane: CH3CH2CHBrCH3 (Markovnikov addition)
(iii) Cyclohexene + HBr → Bromocyclohexane: C6H11Br

(b) (i) C3H8 + 5O2 → 3CO2 + 4H2O
(ii) C3H8 + 3H2O → 3CO + 7H2 (steam reforming)
(iii) C6H10 + H2 → C6H12 (cyclohexane)

subject: Chemistry
question:
(c) Write a structural formula for each of the following and indicate whether it is primary, secondary or tertiary.
(i) 3-pentanol
(ii) 2,2-dimethyl-1-propanol
(iii) 1-methyl cyclopentanol
(iv) 2-methyl-2-propanol [4 marks]
modelAnswer:
(i) 3-pentanol: CH3CH2CHOHCH2CH3 - Secondary alcohol
(ii) 2,2-dimethyl-1-propanol: (CH3)3CCH2OH - Primary alcohol
(iii) 1-methyl cyclopentanol: Five-membered ring with OH and CH3 on same carbon - Tertiary alcohol
(iv) 2-methyl-2-propanol: (CH3)3COH - Tertiary alcohol

subject: Chemistry
question:
(a) Explain the following terms:
(i) Precision
(ii) Accuracy [4 marks]

(b) The concentration of Nickel in Nigerian coin was determined with visible spectrophotometer, and the following results (%) were obtained: 3.65, 4.11, 3.59, 7.51, 3.95, 3.87, 4.06, 1.48, 3.60, 3.76 and 3.99. If the true concentration (%) of nickel in coin as determined by atomic absorption and inductively coupled plasma atomic emission spectrophotometer was 3.92. Use the above data to determine:
(i) Absolute error
(ii) Percentage relative error
(iii) Average error [6 marks]
modelAnswer:
(a) Precision: Closeness of repeated measurements to each other (reproducibility). Accuracy: Closeness of a measurement to the true value.

(b) Mean = (3.65+4.11+3.59+7.51+3.95+3.87+4.06+1.48+3.60+3.76+3.99)/11 = 43.57/11 = 3.96
(i) Absolute error = |3.96 - 3.92| = 0.04
(ii) Percentage relative error = (0.04/3.92) × 100 = 1.02%
(iii) Average error = (0.31+0.15+0.37+3.55+0.01+0.09+0.10+2.48+0.36+0.20+0.03)/11 = 7.65/11 = 0.70

subject: Chemistry
question:
(a) Name the strongest type of intermolecular force between hydrogen fluoride molecules and draw a diagram to illustrate how two molecules of HF are attracted to each other. In your diagram show all lone pairs of electrons and any partial charges. Explain the origin of these charges. Suggest why this strong intermolecular force is not present between HI molecules. [3 marks]

(b) Crystals of sodium chloride and of diamond both have giant structures. Their melting points are 1074K and 3827K respectively. State the type of structure present in each case and explain why the melting point of diamond is so high. [3 marks]

(c) Write a Lewis structure and identify the octet-rule exception for:
(i) SClF5
(ii) BFCl2 [4 marks]
modelAnswer:
(a) Hydrogen bonding. Partial charges arise from fluorine's high electronegativity. HI does not show strong hydrogen bonding because iodine is less electronegative and has larger size.

(b) NaCl: Ionic giant structure. Diamond: Covalent network giant structure. Diamond's melting point is higher because covalent bonds extend throughout the crystal and are very strong.

(c) (i) SClF5: Sulfur has 12 electrons (expanded octet - uses d-orbitals)
(ii) BFCl2: Boron has 6 electrons (incomplete octet - electron deficient)

subject: Chemistry
question:
(a) State Gay-Lussac's law [2 marks]

(b) State Afbau's principle and Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity. [2 marks]

(c) What do you understand by a Bronsted acid? [1 mark]

(d) Briefly explain the term 'Inert Pair Effect'. [2 marks]

(e) Compare and contrast the following properties of the transition elements with that of S and P-block elements.
(i) Colour
(ii) Atomic / ionic radius
(iii) Oxidation states. [3 marks]
modelAnswer:
(a) Gay-Lussac's law: At constant volume, pressure of a fixed mass of gas is directly proportional to absolute temperature (P ∝ T).

(b) Aufbau principle: Electrons fill orbitals in order of increasing energy. Hund's rule: Electrons occupy degenerate orbitals singly with parallel spins before pairing.

(c) A Brønsted acid is a substance that donates a proton (H⁺).

(d) Inert pair effect: Heavier p-block elements show oxidation states two less than group valency because s-electrons become inert (e.g., Tl⁺ more stable than Tl³⁺).

(e) (i) Colour: Transition elements form coloured compounds (d-d transitions); S/P-block compounds are usually colourless.
(ii) Radius: Transition elements have smaller radii than s-block; radii decrease gradually across period.
(iii) Oxidation states: Transition elements show multiple variable oxidation states; S/P-block typically have fixed oxidation states.

subject: Chemistry
question: (a) Write an equation for the synthesis of hydrogen gas by the reaction of methane with steam. [1 mark]
modelAnswer: CH₄(g) + H₂O(g) → CO(g) + 3H₂(g) (steam reforming of methane)

subject: Chemistry
question: (b) Compare the physical and chemical properties of the hydrides of Na, C, N and S. [3 marks]
modelAnswer: NaH: Ionic solid, reacts violently with water (strong base). CH₄: Covalent gas, non-polar, does not react with water. NH₃: Covalent gas, polar, soluble in water (weak base). H₂S: Covalent gas, polar, soluble in water (weak acid). Trend: Ionic → covalent; reactivity with water decreases; basicity decreases.

subject: Chemistry
question:
(c) Explain using equation why rain falling in unpolluted air is acidic with a pH of about 5.6. [2 marks]

(d) Acid rain has a pH value less than 5.6. Explain using equation how the burning of coal can contribute to acid rain formation. [2 marks]
modelAnswer:
(c) CO₂(g) + H₂O(l) ⇌ H₂CO₃(aq); H₂CO₃ ⇌ H⁺ + HCO₃⁻ (carbonic acid formation)

(d) S + O₂ → SO₂; 2SO₂ + O₂ → 2SO₃; SO₃ + H₂O → H₂SO₄ (sulfuric acid formation)

subject: Chemistry
question: (e) What are the consequences of global warming on the environment? [2 marks]
modelAnswer:
1. Rising sea levels (melting ice caps)
2. Extreme weather events (hurricanes, droughts, floods)
3. Loss of biodiversity
4. Agricultural disruption
5. Ocean acidification

subject: Chemistry
question:
An organic compound X contains 40% carbon, 53.3% oxygen and 6.7% hydrogen. The vapor density of X is 30. The organic compound reacted with sodium hydrogen trioxocarbonate (IV) and a colorless gas was given off which turned lime water milky. On boiling under reflux, the mixture of X and ethanol, a sweet smelling liquid Y was formed.

(a) Determine the:
(i) empirical formula of X
(ii) molecular formula of X [4 marks]

(b) What is the name of the colourless gas given off? [1 mark]

(c) Give the formula of the functional group present in X. [1 mark]

(d) Deduce the structure of the compound X. [1 mark]

(e) Name the reaction which took place between X and ethanol, and write an equation for the reaction between X and ethanol. [3 marks]
modelAnswer:
(a) (i) C:H:O = 40/12 : 6.7/1 : 53.3/16 = 3.33:6.7:3.33 = 1:2:1. Empirical formula = CH₂O
(ii) Molar mass = 2 × 30 = 60 g/mol. n = 60/30 = 2. Molecular formula = C₂H₄O₂

(b) Carbon dioxide (CO₂)

(c) Carboxyl group (-COOH)

(d) CH₃COOH (ethanoic acid)

(e) Esterification (Fischer esterification). Equation: CH₃COOH + C₂H₅OH ⇌ CH₃COOC₂H₅ + H₂O

subject: Chemistry
question:
(a) State three (3) major factors which affect the discharge of ions at the electrodes during electrolysis. [3 marks]

(b) Explain why a tin-plated iron container corrodes faster than a galvanized iron container with reference to the electrochemical series. [2 marks]

(c) 528g of a trivalent metal is deposited when a current of 1.56A is passed through a solution of its salt for 1 hour; calculate the relative atomic mass of the metal. Hence identify the metal. (1F = 96500C) [2 marks]

(d) State any three (3) common applications of electrolysis. [3 marks]
modelAnswer:
(a) 1. Position of ions in electrochemical series (lower E° discharged first)
2. Concentration of ions (higher concentration favored)
3. Nature of electrode material

(b) Tin is less reactive (more noble) than iron, so iron corrodes when coating is scratched. Zinc is more reactive than iron, so zinc corrodes sacrificially, protecting iron.

(c) Q = It = 1.56 × 3600 = 5616 C. Moles e⁻ = 5616/96500 = 0.0582 mol. For M³⁺, moles M = 0.0582/3 = 0.0194 mol. Atomic mass = 0.528/0.0194 = 27.2 g/mol. Metal is Aluminium (Al).

(d) 1. Electroplating
2. Extraction of metals (e.g., aluminium)
3. Purification of metals (e.g., copper refining)
